post.description: Use this endpoint to create a crypto widget for a session to buy or sell crypto.

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

get.description: Search transaction by its Meld identifier

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the transaction to be fetched

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

404.description: Payment transaction with the id informed was not found

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

get.description: Search a list of transactions you've previously created. The transactions are ordered by the date of creation.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description:
Comma separated list of transaction status.

If present, only transactions with the specified status will be included.

Possible values:

  • PENDING_CREATED
  • PENDING
  • PROCESSING
  • AUTHORIZED
  • AUTHORIZATION_EXPIRED
  • SETTLING
  • ONRAMP_SETTLED
  • SETTLED
  • REFUNDED
  • DECLINED
  • CANCELLED
  • FAILED
  • ERROR
  • VOIDED
  • TWO_FA_REQUIRED
  • TWO_FA_PROVIDED

parameters.name: serviceProviders
parameters.description:
Comma separated list of service providers.

Return only transactions with these specific providers. You can specify multiple service providers in the query string and separate them with commas

Reference Crypto supported service providers and Fiat supported service providers for a full list of supported service providers

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Limits number of returned accounts.

Default value: 10 or the number of ids provided

Minimum value: 1

Maximum value: 100

parameters.name: amountFrom
parameters.description: Return only transactions with the amount equal to or greater than this amount

parameters.name: amountTo
parameters.description: Return only transactions with the amount equal to or less than this amount

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Return only transactions created after this DateTime. The correct string will use the ISO DateTime or ISO Date Format.

Ex:2021-08-31T01:30:00 or 2022-02-25 are accepted

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
Return only transactions created before this DateTime. The correct string will use the ISO DateTime or ISO Date Format.

Ex:2021-08-31T01:30:00 or 2022-02-25 are accepted

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
The unique key corresponding to a payment transaction's position in the paginated list of results -- used to indicate that only payment transactions residing before this key will be retrieved.

Each payment transaction carries a unique key. Reference PaginationThe before and after fields cannot be used in the same request.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
The unique key corresponding to a payment transaction's position in the paginated list of results -- used to indicate that only payment transactions residing after this key will be retrieved.

Each payment transaction carries a unique key. Reference PaginationThe before and after fields cannot be used in the same request.

parameters.name: apiAccessProfileIds
parameters.description: Comma separated list of API access profile ids

parameters.name: customerIds
parameters.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity. This takes a comma separated list of customer ids.

parameters.name: externalCustomerIds
parameters.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity. This takes a comma separated list of external customer ids.

parameters.name: sessionIds
parameters.description: Comma separated list of session ids

parameters.name: externalSessionIds
parameters.description: Comma separated list of external session ids

parameters.name: externalReferenceIds
parameters.description: Comma separated list of reference ids

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Comma separated list of transaction types

parameters.name: orderIds
parameters.description: Comma separated list of order ids (for virtual account ramp)

parameters.name: serviceProviderTransactionId
parameters.description: service provider transaction id

parameters.name: subaccountCustomerIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of sub-account customer ids.

parameters.name: externalSubaccountCustomerIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of external sub-account customer ids.

post.summary: Create a crypto virtual account ramp quote
post.description: Use this endpoint to request the current exchange rate of the selected fiat currency-cryptocurrency pair, and the required fees. Enter a fiat currency as the sourceCurrencyCode to buy crypto and enter a crypto currency in that field to sell crypto.

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

post.summary: Create a virtual account offramp order
post.description: Create a virtual account offramp order to initiate crypto to fiat transfer

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

get.summary: Fetch a transaction from the provider (for offramp use)
get.description: Fetch a transaction from the service provider using the session ID. This endpoint is primarily used for offramp scenarios where the transaction needs to be retrieved from the provider after the user completes their action in the widget.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Session ID to fetch the transaction for

200.description: Transaction found and retrieved from provider

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

404.description: Session not found or transaction not yet created with provider

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

post.description: Use this endpoint to request the current exchange rate of the selected fiat currency-cryptocurrency pair, and the required fees. Enter a fiat currency as the sourceCurrencyCode to buy crypto and enter a crypto currency in that field to sell crypto.

parameters.name: includeSwap
parameters.description: If true, include both direct ramp/offramp quotes and stitched ramp+swap/offramp+swap quotes (CRYPTO_PURCHASE_SWAP / CRYPTO_SELL_SWAP) in the response. When false, swap is only used as a fallback when no direct ramp or offramp providers return quotes. Swap quotes are gated by the per-account onrampSwapEnabled / offrampSwapEnabled preferences regardless of this flag.

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

city.description: City, district, suburb, town, or village

firstName.description: The first name on the address

lastName.description: The last name on the address

lineOne.description: Address line 1 (e.g., street, PO Box, or company name)

lineTwo.description: Address line 2 (e.g., apartment, suite, unit, or building)

type.description: Address type such as BILLING, SHIPPING, RESIDENCE, etc.

addresses.description: Your customer's addresses, such as BILLING, SHIPPING, RESIDENCE, etc.

externalId.description: Your (optional) Id for this customer, for example an id or reference from your database which you may supply during customer creation

id.description: Meld's internal Id for the Customer

customerId.description: Customer id. Required for virtual account ramp quotes.

destinationCurrencyCode.description: Enter a cryptocurrency symbol here if purchasing crypto or a fiat currency 3 letter code if selling crypto.

paymentMethodType.description:
Payment Method Type or SubType

The paymentMethodType and paymentMethodSubType included here are a sample set. Review the [Supported Payment Methods](https://www.meld.io/coverage/payment-methods) section to view the complete list.

serviceProviders.description:
An optional list of Strings representing the Service Providers for this Quote.

Reference Crypto supported service providers for a full list of supported service providers

If this field is null or an empty list, Meld will get quotes from all the service providers linked to the API key used on the request.

sourceAmount.description: The source amount for this transaction.

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Enter a fiat currency 3 letter code here if purchasing crypto or a cryptocurrency symbol if selling crypto.

subdivision.description: subdivision is a optional parameter Only applicable if the country code is US or CA.

destinationAmount.description: Destination currency amount to be received for this quote

destinationAmountWithoutFees.description: The fiat amount exchanged when selling crypto, excluding fees. This will be deprecated in the future.

destinationCurrencyCode.description: Code of currency being quoted: BTC, ETH, ...

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate at the time the request is sent

fiatAmountWithoutFees.description: The fiat amount exchanged when buying or selling crypto, excluding fees.

networkFee.description: The network fee applied to a crypto transaction. To ensure that transactions are processed on cryptocurrency networks, outgoing transactions to external cryptocurrency addresses typically incur a "mining" or "network" fee. This fee is paid to cryptocurrency miners, which are the systems that process the transactions and secure the respective network.

partnerFee.description: The fee amount charged by the partner to process your customer's purchase. This fee % can be set by you.

paymentMethodType.description: Payment Method Type used for this quote

serviceProvider.description: Service Provider used on this quote

sourceAmount.description: Amount requested + fees

sourceAmountWithoutFees.description: The fiat amount being used to buy crypto, excluding fees. This will be deprecated in the future.

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Must be a supported currency

totalFee.description: The total amount of fees that will be charged.

transactionFee.description: The fee amount charged by the service provider to process your customer's purchase

transactionType.description: The type of crypto transaction, which is CRYPTO_PURCHASE or CRYPTO_SELL.

VirtualAccountOnrampOrderRequest.description: Request to create a virtual account onramp order

customerPhoneNumber.description: customer phone number of the individual initiating the order on behalf of the sub account. This is required if passed payment method type requires phone number for example MOBILE_MONEY.

destinationWalletAddress.description: Wallet address to receive the cryptocurrency

externalCustomerId.description: External customer id of the individual initiating the order

externalSubaccountCustomerId.description: Optional external identifier for the sub-account customer this transaction belongs to. Used for tracking when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account. A sub-account customer must be created first before it can be referenced.

paymentMethodType.description: The desired payment method type in order to send the fiat currency

serviceProvider.description: Service provider to use for this onramp order

sourceAmount.description: Source amount in fiat currency

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Source currency code

subaccountCustomerId.description: Optional Meld customer id used to track the business sub-account this transaction belongs to when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account.

ReceivingBankInformation.description: Receiving bank details. The schema depends on paymentMethodType.

VirtualAccountOnrampOrderResponse.description: Response containing virtual account onramp order details and bank information

orderId.description: Unique order ID for tracking the onramp order

paymentMethodType.description: Payment method type for the bank transfer

receivingBankInformation.description: Bank information for receiving the fiat transfer; shape depends on paymentMethodType

serviceProviderDetails.description: The raw service provider details for the order

AchPaymentMethodDetails.description: Payment method details for ACH / LOCAL_BANK_TRANSFER

owner.description: Account owner's first and last name

PaymentMethodDetails.description: Payment method details. The schema depends on paymentMethod.type.

VirtualAccountOfframpOrderRequest.description: Request to create a virtual account offramp order

externalSubaccountCustomerId.description: Optional external identifier for the sub-account customer this transaction belongs to. Used for tracking when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account. A sub-account customer must be created first before it can be referenced.

paymentMethod.description: Payment method details for how and where the fiat currency will be received

serviceProvider.description: Service provider to use for this offramp order

sourceAmount.description: Source amount in cryptocurrency

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Source cryptocurrency code

sourceWalletAddress.description: Source wallet address to send cryptocurrency from

subaccountCustomerId.description: Optional Meld customer id used to track the business sub-account this transaction belongs to when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account.

VirtualAccountOfframpOrderResponse.description: Response containing virtual account offramp order details

orderId.description: Unique order ID for tracking the offramp order

paymentMethodType.description: Payment method for receiving the fiat funds

walletAddress.description: Wallet address used for the transfer

PaymentMethodInput.description: PaymentMethod objects represent your customer's payment instruments

type.description: Payment method type used for this transaction, some methods may only apply for some providers, e.g. PLAID_TRANSFER_AUTHORIZATION for PLAID

capture.description: Should the payment be captured immediately, if set to false an authorization will be performed instead

customerId.description: Meld's internal Id for the Customer

externalCustomerId.description: Your (optional) Id for this customer, for example an id or reference from your database which you may supply during customer creation

nonce.description: A payment method nonce is a secure, one-time-use reference to payment information. It's the key element that allows your server to communicate sensitive payment information to the service provider without ever touching the raw data.

paymentMethod.description: PaymentMethod objects represent your customer's payment instruments.

serviceProvider.description: The service provider used for this transaction. Required when using a nonce or passthrough credentials, as they are tied to a specific service provider. Do not pass this in if you would like to use routing to select a service provider.

serviceProviderDetails.description: Provider specific details which may can be provided for this transaction, if supported by the Service Provider. For example, you can set a custom billing descriptor for your transaction using Checkout.com

sourceAmount.description: Amount intended to be collected by this payment

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase. Must be a supported currency

blockchainTransactionId.description: The transaction id on the blockchain

chainId.description: A unique identifier that represents a blockchain network. The chainId of a particular network is standard everywhere.

institution.description: The institution used to execute the transaction. Only applicable for transfers currently.

networkFee.description: This fee is paid to cryptocurrency miners, which are the systems that process the transactions

swapFee.description: The swap fee amount charged by the swap provider for ramp+swap transactions

totalFee.description: The total amount of fees that will be charged

transactionFee.description: The fee amount charged by the service provider to process your customer's purchase

type.description: Address type such as BILLING, SHIPPING, RESIDENCE, etc.

addresses.description: Your Customer's addresses, such as billing, shipping, etc.

externalId.description: Your optionally supplied Id for your Customer, e.g. a Customer Id in your system

id.description: Meld's internal Id for the Customer

serviceProviders.description: A map of Service Providers and service provider IDs for this Customer

status.description: ACTIVE or INACTIVE, may not be set

method.description: How the card details were authorized

submitted.description: Return True if the information was submitted

successful.description: Return True if the submitted information is a match

url.description: URL used to perform authorization (if applicable)

authAmount.description: The amount authorized to be charged (must be greater than the amount intended to be collected.) Note that the number of digits with a decimal point varies with the currency. Reference the list of active codes of official ISO 4217 currency names.

captureAmount.description: The amount captured (can be less than the amount intended to be collected.) Note that the number of digits with a decimal point varies with the currency. Reference the list of active codes of official ISO 4217 currency names.

createdAt.description: An ISO 8601 DateTime at which the object was created.

cryptoDetails.description: Details of the crypto currency purchase, sale, or transfer

description.description: An arbitrary string which you can attach to this transaction. It is displayed when in the web interface alongside the charge.

destinationAmount.description: The destination amount of the transaction, e.g. the amount of crypto currency purchased

destinationCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Must be a supported currency

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

externalReferenceId.description: Your optional reference id provided during transaction creation

externalSessionId.description: The external Id of the session.

externalSubaccountCustomerId.description: Optional external identifier for the sub-account customer this transaction belongs to. Used for tracking when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account. A sub-account customer must be created first before it can be referenced.

fiatAmountInUsd.description: The fiat amount of the transaction in usd

isImported.description: Return True if this transaction was initiated outside Meld

isPassthrough.description: Return True if the passthrough credentials were used

key.description: Pagination key, may be used with the "after" parameter in search requests.  Only set and relevant for paginated calls

multiFactorAuthorizationStatus.description: The transaction required multi-factor authentication methods, and their current status. When required, must be approved for the purchase tobe completed successfully.

orderId.description: Unique identifier for the order associated with this transaction, when applicable

parentPaymentTransactionId.description: The Id of the associated object for this line item

passthroughReference.description: The unique reference you use to identify the partner on whose behalf you created this transaction.  This might be a String or UUID in your system to identify a merchant or partner.  This is needed so that you can associate passthrough transactions to your partners who may not be registered with Meld

paymentMethodType.description: Payment Method type used for this transaction.

serviceProvider.description: The service provider used for this transaction

serviceProviderCreatedAt.description: The ISO 8601 DateTime when the transaction was created on the service provider's side

serviceProviderTransactionUrl.description: A link to the resource on the Service Provider, may not be present

serviceTransactionId.description: The service provider's unique identifier for this transaction

sourceAmount.description: The amount of the transaction

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Must be a supported currency

status.description: The current status of the transaction

subaccountCustomerId.description: Optional Meld customer id used to track the business sub-account this transaction belongs to when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account.

updatedAt.description: Time at which the object was last updated. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

description.description: An arbitrary string attached to the object. Often useful for displaying to users.

sourceAmount.description: Amount to be refunded. Can be a partial refund. If not provided, a refund for the total value will be submitted

paymentMethodType.description:
Payment Method Type

The paymentMethodType and paymentMethodSubType included here are a sample set. Review the [Supported Payment Methods](https://www.meld.io/coverage/payment-methods) section to view the complete list.

redirectFlow.description: The user is redirected back to the wallet, and the wallet gets the amount, token, and wallet address to send money to from the SP's API, then queues up a transfer in the wallet UI and the user just hits confirm

redirectUrl.description: The url to redirect to following completion of the widget flow

refundWalletAddress.description: Optional wallet address where the swap provider sends refunds if the swap fails. Only applicable for ramp+swap (e.g. fiat->USDC->USDH) sessions.

serviceProvider.description:
The service provider used for this transaction

Reference Crypto supported service providers for a full list of supported service providers

sourceAmount.description: The amount of source currency your customer wants tosend in this transaction (e.g., 50.05)

sourceCurrencyCode.description: The fiat currency the user is buying crypto with (e.g. USD, EUR) or the cryptocurrency the user is selling (e.g. BTC, ETH).

walletAddress.description: Your customer's wallet address. This field is required when buying crypto but optional when selling crypto.

walletTag.description: Your customer's wallet tag/destination tag/memo. This field is required for certain cryptocurrencies.

bypassKyc.description: When true, the system will automatically look up the customer's KYC status and generate a share token if KYC is approved. Requires a valid customerId or externalCustomerId.

customerScore.description: A numerical score representing the expectation a Customer will convert. Read more about this feature here.

destinationAmount.description: Destination currency amount to be received for this quote

destinationAmountWithoutFees.description: The fiat amount exchanged when selling crypto, excluding fees. This will be deprecated in the future.

destinationCurrencyCode.description: Code of currency being quoted: BTC, ETH, ...

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate at the time the request is sent

fiatAmountWithoutFees.description: The fiat amount exchanged when buying or selling crypto, excluding fees.

institutionName.description: The name of the institution or broker used for this quote, if present

isNativeAvailable.description: Indicates if native Apple Pay flow is available for this quote (Mercuryo specific)

lowKyc.description: LowKYC indicates if the quote amount is below the provider's low KYC threshold, meaning the user may not need to upload documents, which can increase conversion. Read more about this feature here.

networkFee.description: The network fee applied to a crypto transaction. To ensure that transactions are processed on cryptocurrency networks, outgoing transactions to external cryptocurrency addresses typically incur a "mining" or "network" fee. This fee is paid to cryptocurrency miners, which are the systems that process the transactions and secure the respective network.

partnerFee.description: The fee amount charged by the partner to process your customer's purchase. This fee % can be set by you.

paymentMethodType.description: Payment Method Type used for this quote

serviceProvider.description: Service Provider used on this quote

sourceAmount.description: Amount requested + fees

sourceAmountWithoutFees.description: The fiat amount being used to buy crypto, excluding fees. This will be deprecated in the future.

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Must be a supported currency

totalFee.description: The total amount of fees that will be charged.

transactionFee.description: The fee amount charged by the service provider to process your customer's purchase

transactionType.description: The type of crypto transaction, which is CRYPTO_PURCHASE or CRYPTO_SELL.

serviceProvider.description: The service provider that this event is coming from

serviceProviderEventId.description: The service provider's ID for the given event, for example transaction ID for transaction events

serviceProviderEventPayload.description: JSON payload of the widget event, for reference only, can't fully trust content sent from FE

serviceProviderEventType.description: The service provider's type for the given event

cryptoCurrency.description: The crypto currency for which the DCA information is requested

currencyCode.description: A string representing the currency code. (e.g.: 'USD'

serviceProvider.description: A String representing the provider where this transactions was created (e.g.: BRAINTREE

walletAddress.description: The wallet address for which the DCA information is request

description.description: The code that needs to be sent in the description field.

iban.description: The IBAN where the fiat need to be sent to.

HeadlessOrderRequest.description: Request to create a headless onramp order

customerId.description: Meld customer id (one of customerId / externalCustomerId is required)

customerPhoneNumber.description: Customer phone number — required for MOBILE_MONEY

destinationWalletAddress.description: Wallet address to receive the cryptocurrency

externalCustomerId.description: Caller's external customer id (one of customerId / externalCustomerId is required)

externalSessionId.description: Caller's reference for the order; unique per account when provided

paymentMethodType.description: Payment method type

providerData.description: Provider-specific extension; subtype keyed by serviceProvider

redirectUrl.description: Redirect URL — required for CARD/REDIRECT; validated against headlessConfig.allowedRedirectUrls

serviceProvider.description: Service provider to use for the order

sourceAmount.description: Source amount

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Source currency code (ISO 4217)

serviceProviderWidgetUrl.description: Short-TTL provider URL; caller opens at user tap

ApplePayOrder.description: Apple Pay response — present when paymentMethodType == APPLE_PAY

BankTransferOrder.description: Bank-transfer response — present for ACH, SEPA, PIX, MOBILE_MONEY

renderMode.description: Render mode for the card surface (NPP-sourced)

CardOrder.description: Card-method response — present when paymentMethodType == CREDIT_DEBIT_CARD

paymentMethodType.description: Payment method type — also acts as the discriminator for paymentMethodResponseDetails

status.description: The status of the access profile

remaining.description: Number of payment transactions before/after this page, depending the direction this page was selected.

apiAccessProfileId.description: The id of the API access profile used to create this transaction. May be null if the transaction was not created via an endpoint that uses an API key for authentication. For example, when imported as a result of a webhook from a service provider.

authAmount.description: The amount authorized to be charged (must be greater than the amount intended to be collected.) Note that the number of digits with a decimal point varies with the currency. Reference the list of active codes of official ISO 4217 currency names.

captureAmount.description: The amount captured (can be less than the amount intended to be collected.) Note that the number of digits with a decimal point varies with the currency. Reference the list of active codes of official ISO 4217 currency names.

createdAt.description: An ISO 8601 DateTime at which the object was created.

cryptoPurchaseDetails.description: Details of the crypto currency purchase

description.description: An arbitrary string which you can attach to this transaction. It is displayed when in the web interface alongside the charge.

destinationAmount.description: The destination amount of the transaction, e.g. the amount of crypto currency purchased

destinationCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Must be a supported currency

destinationWalletAddress.description: The wallet the crypto currency is being deposited in, if known

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

externalReferenceId.description: Your optional reference id provided during transaction creation

externalSessionId.description: The external Id of the session.

externalSubaccountCustomerId.description: Optional external identifier for the sub-account customer this transaction belongs to. Used for tracking when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account. A sub-account customer must be created first before it can be referenced.

isImported.description: Return True if this transaction was initiated outside Meld

isPassthrough.description: Return True if the passthrough credentials were used

key.description: Pagination key, may be used with the "after" parameter in search requests.  Only set and relevant for paginated calls

multiFactorAuthorizationStatus.description: The transaction required multi-factor authentication methods, and their current status. When required, must be approved for the purchase tobe completed successfully.

parentPaymentTransactionId.description: The Id of the associated object for this line item

passthroughReference.description: The unique reference you use to identify the partner on whose behalf you created this transaction.  This might be a String or UUID in your system to identify a merchant or partner.  This is needed so that you can associate passthrough transactions to your partners who may not be registered with Meld

paymentMethod.description: PaymentMethod objects represent your customer's payment instruments.

paymentMethodType.description: Payment Method type used for this transaction.

serviceProvider.description: The service provider used for this transaction

serviceProviderCreatedAt.description: The ISO 8601 DateTime when the transaction was created on the service provider's side

serviceProviderTransactionUrl.description: A link to the resource on the Service Provider, may not be present

serviceTransactionId.description: The service provider's unique identifier for this transaction

sourceAmount.description: The amount of the transaction

sourceAmountInUsd.description: The amount of the transaction in usd

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Must be a supported currency

sourceWalletAddress.description: The wallet the crypto currency is being withdrawn from, if known

status.description: The current status of the transaction

subaccountCustomerId.description: Optional Meld customer id used to track the business sub-account this transaction belongs to when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account.

updatedAt.description: Time at which the object was last updated. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

wallet.description: The wallet used for a crypto currency transaction, if known

serviceProviderCustomerId.description: May be null if the customer has not been created yet or could not be created. See the serviceProviderDetails for more information.

defaultAmount.description: The default amount the crypto wizard shows for the fiat currency.

maxAmount.description: The maximum amount a quote can be requested for, regardless of cryptocurrency.

minAmount.description: The minimum amount a quote can be requested for, regardless of cryptocurrency.

customerId.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

externalTransactionId.description: Your reference id for this transaction

id.description: Unique identifier for this transaction, may be null until transaction is created

serviceProvider.description: The service provider used for this transaction.

serviceProviderDetails.description: Provider specific details which may can be provided for this transaction, if supported by the Service Provider. For example, you can set a custom billing descriptor for your transaction using Checkout.com

sourceAmount.description: Amount intended to be collected by this payment

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase. Must be a supported currency

status.description: The current status of the transaction, may be null until transaction is created

customerId.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

destinationCurrencyCode.description: The cryptocurrency the user is buying (e.g. BTC, ETH) or the fiat currency the user is selling crypto for (e.g. USD, EUR).

isNativeApplePayAvailable.description: Whether native Apple Pay is available for this session (provider eligibility signal). Present for Apple Pay sessions when supported.

lockFields.description: Where supported by the Service Provider, Meld can help you lock the cryptocurrency and/or wallet address fields within the widget. Enable this by passing in the "cryptoCurrency" and/or "walletAddress" as strings in this field. See this page for more details.

paymentMethodType.description:
Payment Method Type

The paymentMethodType and paymentMethodSubType included here are a sample set. Review the [Supported Payment Methods](https://www.meld.io/coverage/payment-methods) section to view the complete list.

serviceProvider.description:
The service provider used for this transaction

Reference Crypto supported service providers for a full list of supported service providers

sessionType.description: The type for this Session. This is only returned, not read. This is inferred from the source and destination currencies used to create the session.

sourceAmount.description: The amount of source currency your customer wants tosend in this transaction (e.g., 50.05)

sourceCurrencyCode.description: The fiat currency the user is buying crypto with (e.g. USD, EUR) or the cryptocurrency the user is selling (e.g. BTC, ETH).

sourceCurrencyCodes.description: List of crypto currencies available for the customer to transfer (e.g. BTC, ETH).

walletAddress.description: Your customer's wallet address. This field is required when buying crypto but optional when selling crypto.

walletTag.description: Your customer's wallet tag/destination tag/memo. This field is required for certain cryptocurrencies.

remaining.description: Number of payment transactions before/after this page, depending the direction this page was selected.

apiAccessProfileId.description: The id of the API access profile used to create this transaction. May be null if the transaction was not created via an endpoint that uses an API key for authentication. For example, when imported as a result of a webhook from a service provider.

createdAt.description: An ISO 8601 DateTime at which the object was created.

cryptoDetails.description: Details of the crypto currency purchase, sale, or transfer

description.description: An arbitrary string which you can attach to this transaction. It is displayed when in the web interface alongside the charge.

destinationAmount.description: The destination amount of the transaction, e.g. the amount of crypto currency purchased

destinationCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Must be a supported currency

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

externalReferenceId.description: Your optional reference id provided during transaction creation

externalSessionId.description: The external Id of the session.

externalSubaccountCustomerId.description: Optional external identifier for the sub-account customer this transaction belongs to. Used for tracking when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account. A sub-account customer must be created first before it can be referenced.

fiatAmountInUsd.description: The fiat amount of the transaction in usd

isImported.description: Return True if this transaction was initiated outside Meld

isPassthrough.description: Return True if the passthrough credentials were used

key.description: Pagination key, may be used with the "after" parameter in search requests.  Only set and relevant for paginated calls

multiFactorAuthorizationStatus.description: The transaction required multi-factor authentication methods, and their current status. When required, must be approved for the purchase tobe completed successfully.

orderId.description: Unique identifier for the order associated with this transaction, when applicable

parentPaymentTransactionId.description: The Id of the associated object for this line item

passthroughReference.description: The unique reference you use to identify the partner on whose behalf you created this transaction.  This might be a String or UUID in your system to identify a merchant or partner.  This is needed so that you can associate passthrough transactions to your partners who may not be registered with Meld

paymentMethodType.description: Payment Method type used for this transaction.

serviceProvider.description: The service provider used for this transaction

serviceProviderCreatedAt.description: The ISO 8601 DateTime when the transaction was created on the service provider's side

serviceProviderTransactionUrl.description: A link to the resource on the Service Provider, may not be present

serviceTransactionId.description: The service provider's unique identifier for this transaction

sourceAmount.description: The amount of the transaction

sourceCurrencyCode.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Must be a supported currency

status.description: The current status of the transaction

subaccountCustomerId.description: Optional Meld customer id used to track the business sub-account this transaction belongs to when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account.

updatedAt.description: Time at which the object was last updated. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

destinationCurrencyCode.description: The cryptocurrency the customer is buying (e.g. BTC, ETH).

institutionId.description: If provided, the institution to bypass the institution selection screen in the service provider's widget. Currently this is only supported for MESH widget sessions.

kycInfo.description: Optional KYC information for token passthrough. When provided with a valid KYC token from a supported provider, the verification can be transferred to downstream providers that support it.

lockFields.description: Where supported by the Service Provider, Meld can help you lock the cryptocurrency and/or wallet address fields within the widget. Enable this by passing in the "destinationCurrencyCode" and/or "walletAddress" as strings in this field. See this page for more details.

paymentMethodType.description:
Payment Method Type

The paymentMethodType and paymentMethodSubType included here are a sample set. Review the [Supported Payment Methods](https://www.meld.io/coverage/payment-methods) section to view the complete list.

redirectUrl.description: The url to redirect to following completion of the widget flow

refundWalletAddress.description: Optional wallet address where the swap provider sends refunds if the swap fails. Only applicable for ramp+swap (e.g. fiat->USDC->USDH) sessions.

serviceProvider.description:
The service provider used for this transaction

Reference Crypto supported service providers for a full list of supported service providers

sourceAmount.description: The amount of source currency your customer wants tosend in this transaction (e.g., 50.05)

sourceCurrencyCode.description: The fiat currency the user is buying crypto with (e.g. USD, EUR).

walletTag.description: Your customer's wallet tag/destination tag/memo. This field is required for certain cryptocurrencies.

destinationCurrencyCode.description: The fiat currency the customer is selling crypto for (e.g. USD, EUR).

kycInfo.description: Optional KYC information for token passthrough. When provided with a valid KYC token from a supported provider, the verification can be transferred to downstream providers that support it.

lockFields.description: Where supported by the Service Provider, Meld can help you lock the cryptocurrency and/or wallet address fields within the widget. Enable this by passing in the "destinationCurrencyCode" and/or "walletAddress" as strings in this field. See this page for more details.

paymentMethodType.description:
Payment Method Type

The paymentMethodType and paymentMethodSubType included here are a sample set. Review the [Supported Payment Methods](https://www.meld.io/coverage/payment-methods) section to view the complete list.

redirectFlow.description: The user is redirected back to the wallet, and the wallet gets the amount, token, and wallet address to send money to from the SP’s API, then queues up a transfer in the wallet UI and the user just hits confirm

redirectUrl.description: The url to redirect to following completion of the widget flow

refundWalletAddress.description: Optional wallet address where the swap provider sends refunds if the swap fails. Only applicable for sell+swap (e.g. USDH->USDC->fiat) sessions. Defaults to walletAddress if not provided.

serviceProvider.description:
The service provider used for this transaction

Reference Crypto supported service providers for a full list of supported service providers

sourceAmount.description: The amount of source currency your customer wants tosend in this transaction (e.g., 50.05)

sourceCurrencyCode.description: The cryptocurrency the customer is selling (e.g. BTC, ETH).

walletAddress.description: Your customer's wallet address. Not required for selling crypto.

walletTag.description: Your customer's wallet tag/destination tag/memo. This field is required for certain cryptocurrencies.

institutionId.description: If provided, the institution to bypass the institution selection screen in the service provider's widget. Currently this is only supported for MESH widget sessions.

kycInfo.description: Optional KYC information for token passthrough. When provided with a valid KYC token from a supported provider, the verification can be transferred to downstream providers that support it.

lockFields.description: Where supported by the Service Provider, Meld can help you lock the cryptocurrency and/or wallet address fields within the widget. Enable this by passing in the "destinationCurrencyCode" and/or "walletAddress" as strings in this field. See this page for more details.

redirectUrl.description: The url to redirect to following completion of the widget flow

serviceProvider.description:
The service provider used for this transaction

Reference Crypto supported service providers for a full list of supported service providers

sourceAmount.description: The amount of source currency your customer wants totransfer (e.g., 50.05). It only applies if one source currency code is passed in.

sourceCurrencyCodes.description: Allowable crypto currencies for the customer to transfer (e.g. BTC, ETH). If the currency to transfer is already known, then this list should be singular and the customer will not have the option to select a different crypto currency in the service provider's widget. Alternatively, if the currency to transfer is not yet known and it is desired that the customer select it themselves in the service provider widget, then this list will restrict which crypto currencies are made available. However, this latter option of passing in multiple currencies is only supported by select service providers (MESH, ROBINHOOD). At least one currency must be passed in. The accompanying sourceAmount field should only be present if a single crypto currency is passed in.

walletTag.description: Your customer's wallet tag/destination tag/memo. This field is required for certain cryptocurrencies.

kycInfo.description: Optional KYC information for token passthrough. When provided with a valid KYC token from a supported provider, the verification can be transferred to downstream providers that support it.

lockFields.description: Where supported by the Service Provider, Meld can help you lock the cryptocurrency and/or wallet address fields within the widget. Enable this by passing in the "destinationCurrencyCode" and/or "walletAddress" as strings in this field. See this page for more details.

redirectUrl.description: The url to redirect to following completion of the widget flow

serviceProvider.description:
The service provider used for this transaction

Reference Crypto supported service providers for a full list of supported service providers

walletTag.description: Your customer's wallet tag/destination tag/memo. This field is required for certain cryptocurrencies.

bypassKyc.description: When true, the system will automatically look up the customer's KYC status and generate a share token if KYC is approved. Requires a valid customerId or externalCustomerId.

customerId.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

externalSessionId.description: Your reference id for your widget session, if you use this optional field you must pass in a unique value each time

externalSubaccountCustomerId.description: Optional external identifier for the sub-account customer this transaction belongs to. Used for tracking when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account. A sub-account customer must be created first before it can be referenced.

sessionType.description: The type of widget session to create

subaccountCustomerId.description: Optional Meld customer id used to track the business sub-account this transaction belongs to when multiple businesses operate under one Meld account.

blockchainTransactionId.description: The transaction id on the blockchain

chainId.description: A unique identifier that represents a blockchain network. The chainId of a particular network is standard everywhere.

destinationWalletAddress.description: The wallet address that the crypto is sent to. This is usually the user's wallet address for buy and transfer transactions, and the onramp's wallet address for sell transactions.

institution.description: The institution used to execute the transaction. Only applicable for transfers currently.

networkFee.description: This fee is paid to cryptocurrency miners, which are the systems that process the transactions

sessionWalletAddress.description: The wallet address passed into the /widget endpoint, typically of the user. This will often be the same as either the sourceWalletAddress or the destinationWalletAddress.

sourceWalletAddress.description: The wallet address that the crypto comes from. This is usually the onramp's wallet address for buy transactions, and the user's wallet address for transfer and sell transactions.

swapFee.description: The swap fee amount charged by the swap provider for ramp+swap transactions

totalFee.description: The total amount of fees that will be charged

transactionFee.description: The fee amount charged by the service provider to process your customer's purchase

remaining.description: Number of payment transactions before/after this page, depending the direction this page was selected.

networkFee.description: The network fee applied to a crypto transaction. To ensure that transactions are processed on cryptocurrency networks, outgoing transactions to external cryptocurrency addresses typically incur a "mining" or "network" fee. rampIntelligence.description: A collection of information on how to rank the quotes returned to you, based on Meld's scoring algorithms. Read more about this here.

previouslyUsed.description: Timestamp of previously successful purchase for this customer on this ramp

rampScore.description: A numerical score representing the expectation a Customer will convert (Ramp score). Read more about this feature here.
